summary: NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler implementing three-path lifecycle (set/nil+absent/nil+exists) with auto-derived egress for DNS/MariaDB/Memcached, CEL validation, RBAC marker, Owns watch, and unit tests. Two blocking issues: CRD YAML not regenerated after types change, and Helm ClusterRole missing networking.k8s.io RBAC rule. One major issue: ensureNetworkPolicy always calls c.Update without snapshot comparison, violating the established EnsureHPA/EnsurePDB pattern.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./... passes)
buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy unit tests cover pod selector, labels, policy types, ingress, egress auto-derivation, DNS, additionalEgress
Lifecycle tests cover create, update, nil paths, ObservedGeneration, error propagation
Edge cases covered: brownfield-only (DNS only), multiple ingress sources, pod selector with namespace selector
Error injection via interceptor.Funcs for both Create and Delete errors
Controller-level tests updated for NetworkPolicyReady in all condition lists

code_quality_checklist.item: Error wrapping follows fmt.Errorf('verb context: %%w', err) convention
ensureNetworkPolicy uses snapshot comparison before Update (matches EnsureHPA/EnsurePDB pattern)

security_checklist.item: NetworkPolicy restricts both ingress and egress (PolicyTypes includes both)
Helm ClusterRole template synced with kubebuilder RBAC markers

architecture_checklist.item: Reconciliation chain order correct (after HPA, before Bootstrap)
SetControllerReference on both create and update paths
CRD YAML regenerated after API type changes

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No over-engineering (additionalEgress is required by spec, not speculative)

defensive_checklist.item: Nil checks for spec.networkPolicy before access
CEL XValidation enforces at least one ingress source

security_findings.description: Helm ClusterRole template is missing the networking.k8s.io apiGroup rule for networkpolicies. The kubebuilder RBAC marker was added to keystone_controller.go but the Helm template was not updated. When deployed via Helm, the operator will receive 403 Forbidden on every NetworkPolicy get/create/update/delete call, silently breaking reconciliation.
security_findings.fix: Add a new RBAC rule block after the autoscaling entry: apiGroups: [networking.k8s.io], resources: [networkpolicies], verbs: [get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete] with comment '# networking.k8s.io - networkpolicies (CC-0039)'

architecture_findings.description: CRD YAML (config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml) was not regenerated after adding NetworkPolicySpec, NetworkPolicyIngressSource, and the NetworkPolicy field to keystone_types.go. The CRD schema has no networkPolicy field, no CEL XValidation rule, and no kubebuilder validation markers for the new types. This means the API server will reject any CR with spec.networkPolicy set, or silently strip the field.
architecture_findings.fix: Run 'make generate' and 'make manifests' from operators/keystone/ to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go and the CRD YAML. Commit the generated files.

issues_found.description: CRD YAML not regenerated after API type changes. keystone_types.go adds NetworkPolicySpec, NetworkPolicyIngressSource, and NetworkPolicy *NetworkPolicySpec field, but config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml contains no networkPolicy field. Matches mandatory review pattern 'regenerate-auto-generated-files-after-api-type-changes'.
issues_found.fix: Run 'make generate && make manifests' from operators/keystone/ to regenerate the CRD YAML.

issues_found.description: Helm ClusterRole template missing networking.k8s.io RBAC rule. The kubebuilder marker '+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=networking.k8s.io,resources=networkpolicies,...' was added at keystone_controller.go:63, but the Helm ClusterRole template has no networking.k8s.io entry. Matches mandatory review pattern 'sync-rbac-markers-with-helm-clusterrole-templates'. The operator will get 403 Forbidden at runtime when deployed via Helm.
issues_found.fix:
Add after line 155 (autoscaling block):
  # networking.k8s.io - networkpolicies (CC-0039)
  - apiGroups:
      - networking.k8s.io
    resources:
      - networkpolicies
    verbs:
      - get
      - list
      - watch
      - create
      - update
      - patch
      - delete
issues_found.evidence: grep 'networking.k8s.io' operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml returns no matches, but keystone_controller.go:63 has: // +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=networking.k8s.io,resources=networkpolicies,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete

issues_found.description: ensureNetworkPolicy always calls c.Update without snapshot comparison, violating the established pattern used by EnsurePDB and EnsureHPA. Both EnsurePDB (deployment.go:219) and EnsureHPA (deployment.go:290) use 'before := existing.DeepCopy()' and guard the c.Update call with apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual checks to skip no-op updates. ensureNetworkPolicy at reconcile_networkpolicy.go:217 unconditionally calls c.Update, causing unnecessary API writes, spurious watch events, and potential 409 Conflict errors on every reconciliation.
issues_found.evidence:
EnsureHPA (deployment.go:290): 'before := existing.DeepCopy()' ... 'if !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, before.Spec)' guards Update.
EnsurePDB (deployment.go:219): same pattern.
ensureNetworkPolicy (reconcile_networkpolicy.go:217): 'existing.Spec = np.Spec\nif err := c.Update(ctx, existing); err != nil {' — no guard.

suggested_improvements: Consider moving ensureNetworkPolicy and deleteNetworkPolicy to internal/common/deployment/ package (as EnsureNetworkPolicy/DeleteNetworkPolicy) to align with EnsureHPA, EnsurePDB, and EnsureDeployment. This centralizes the create-or-update pattern and enables reuse by future operators.

DNS egress rule currently allows traffic to any destination on port 53. Consider restricting to kube-system namespace selector if the deployment environment guarantees CoreDNS runs there, matching the review criteria's 'to kube-system' specification.
next_steps: Run 'make generate && make manifests' from operators/keystone/ to regenerate CRD YAML and deepcopy files
Add networking.k8s.io RBAC rule to Helm ClusterRole template (clusterrole.yaml)
Add snapshot-comparison guard (before := existing.DeepCopy() + apiequality check) to ensureNetworkPolicy update path

detected_patterns.name: Optional sub-reconciler with three-path nil-check lifecycle
detected_patterns.applies_when: Implementing a sub-reconciler for an optional spec field (pointer type) that creates a Kubernetes sub-resource when set and deletes it when nil
detected_patterns.description: Optional sub-reconcilers follow a three-path lifecycle: (1) spec field nil → delete any existing sub-resource + set condition True with reason '{Resource}NotRequired', (2) spec field set → build desired resource + ensure (create-or-update) + set condition True with reason '{Resource}Ready', (3) error → propagate wrapped error. The condition type is '{Resource}Ready' and uses ObservedGeneration. The build function is named build{CR}{Resource} and the ensure/delete helpers follow the established Get+Create/Update pattern. Both reconcileHPA (CC-0038) and reconcileNetworkPolicy (CC-0039) follow this structure.
